ocr.text: The following data table presents an analysis of various cytokines, with a specific focus on IL-4, highlighting its impact and
relative expression levels across different PorB variants. Notably, the table includes metrics such as the PorB/VD1 and
PorB/VD2 values, which indicate varying concentrations of IL-4 in response to specific experimental conditions. The
significant differences in these values underscore the cytokine's role in inflammatory responses and its potential implications in

pediatric palliative care, where time demands are notably higher than those observed in adult populations. 

This increased
demand for pediatric palliative care is attributed to the complexities of managing long-term illness trajectories and the necessity
for a holistic approach that encompasses the entire family system.
Cytokine  PorB/VD1  PorB/VD2  PorB/VD3  PorB/VD4  NL PorB
SUBCATEGORY  DESCRIPTION
Time demand  High time demand for paediatric palliative care (in comparison to adult palliative care) due to involvement of the whole family system and longer illness trajectories
Basic conditions of care  Small caseload in paediatric palliative care and a heterogeneous range of conditions bear practical and economical challenges for extensive specialist infrastructure
Challenges for staff  Work in paediatric palliative home care as a "tightrope walk" due to the traumatising impact of the dying of a child and the involvement of the whole family system
Qualification  Requirement of specialist qualification in the field of paediatric hospice and palliative care, as well as supervision and staff support
Locations of care  Advantages of home care ("normality" and intimacy within familiar environment) vs. advantages of inpatient care (safety, relief of medical and nursing responsibility)
ORF  Promoter  5' UTR  3' UTR  Terminator
The corresponding columns, such as ORF and Promoter, provide essential insight into the genetic underpinnings that may
influence cytokine expression and regulation. Understanding these relationships is crucial for developing targeted therapies and
interventions that can effectively address the unique challenges faced in pediatric settings. Therefore, the data presented not
only elucidates the role of IL-4 but also serves as a foundation for further exploration into effective palliative strategies tailored
to the needs of children and their families.
